title: Endodeca hastata
taxonomy: Endodeca hastata is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. Further, it belongs to the tribe Astereae, a group of daisy-like plants characterized by their daisy-like flower heads. The species Endodeca hastata is a perennial herb native to mountainous regions in Central and South America. It is a member of the genus Endodeca, which is part of the broader classification of flowering plants. The classification of Endodeca hastata is still being refined and updated as new research emerges.
uses: Endodeca hastata, also known as American Beauty Berry or Silverleaf, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves and stems are used in herbal remedies to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In addition, the berries of Endodeca hastata are believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant's roots and leaves are also used in some traditional medicine practices to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential benefits of Endodeca hastata.
benefits: Endodeca hastata is also known as American beautyberry or false beautyberry, however, there is limited information available on its health benefits. parts: Endodeca hastata is also known as American feverfew.  The medicinal parts of this plant are the leaves, flowers, and roots.  These parts have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, headaches, and digestive issues.  The plant's bioactive compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.  The leaves and flowers are typically harvested for use in herbal remedies.
preparations: Endodeca hastata is not a well-documented plant in general herbal knowledge.
